---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_15.png){.title-icon} Learning resources"
subtitle: "Courses, books and references for R, epidemiology and statistics"
date: last-modified
---

**If you only pick one:** Start with [DDEA Introductory Course](https://r-cubed-intro.rostools.org/) if you are completely new to R, or [The Epidemiologist R Handbook](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/) if you know a bit of R and want to go straight to health science applications.

## Getting started - learn R

| Resource | What it is |
|---|---|
| ⭐ [DDEA Introductory Course](https://r-cubed-intro.rostools.org/) | Introductory R course from the Danish Diabetes and Endocrine Academy - held once a year, materials freely available. Best starting point if you are completely new. |
| ⭐ [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) | Free e-book by Hadley Wickham - the standard reference for modern R with tidyverse. Concept-based, read from start to finish. |
| [Cookbook for R](http://www.cookbook-r.com) | Task-based reference: "how do I do X?" with direct code examples. Use it when you know what you want but can't remember the syntax. |
| [Quick-R](https://www.statmethods.net) | Compact reference page for R syntax and statistics. Good for quick lookups mid-script. |

## Health science and epidemiology

| Resource | What it is |
|---|---|
| ⭐ [The Epidemiologist R Handbook](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/) | Practical reference written for epidemiologists and health researchers - covers data cleaning, analyses and visualisation with health science examples. |
| ⭐ [Zheers R Coding Café](https://r-coding-cafe.zheer.dk/) | Notes and examples specifically for register data - local to Steno Diabetes Center Aarhus. Most relevant for this study. |
| [CRAN Task View: Epidemiology](https://cran.r-project.org/view=Epidemiology) | Curated package list for epidemiology - incidence rates, matching, cohort analysis. Good starting point when looking for the right package. |
| [CRAN Task View: Survival Analysis](https://cran.r-project.org/view=Survival) | Curated package list for survival analysis - Cox regression, Kaplan–Meier and competing risks. |

**Podcasts:**

| Resource | What it is |
|---|---|
| 🎧 [SERious Epi](https://seriousepi.blubrry.net/) | Podcast on epidemiological methods in practice - study design, bias and analytical choices, discussed by experienced epidemiologists. |
| 🎧 [Causal Inference](https://open.spotify.com/show/1L8TqB17Peo7jNgXuPObwi) | Podcast on causal inference - methods, confounding and causal questions, in an informal conversational format. |

## Methods and statistics

| Resource | What it is |
|---|---|
| [Learning Statistics with R](https://learningstatisticswithr.com/) | Free e-book by Danielle Navarro - covers statistical theory and R implementation side by side, from basic probability to regression and ANOVA. Written for a non-mathematical audience. Relevant if you want to understand the statistics you are running, not just copy the code. |
| [Causal Inference: What If (Hernán & Robins)](https://miguelhernan.org/whatifbook) | Free PDF - the standard reference on causal inference, confounding and DAGs. Epidemiological methods, not R. |
